targets.agent_summary: Reviewed `src/prefilter.rs`, which implements Aho-Corasick prefilter builders and byte-scanning candidates using local `memchr`-based search heuristics. I found no concrete malicious or supply-chain indicators in this file: there are no install hooks, network or exfiltration code, credential access, dynamic code loading, obfuscation/packing, or persistence/environment-tampering behavior.

Reviewed src/packed/mod.rs, which is a documentation-and-reexport module for the packed substring-search API. It contains no install hooks, network or exfiltration code, credential access, dynamic code loading, obfuscation, or persistence behavior.

Reviewed the `COPYING` file, which is a brief dual-license notice for the project under the Unlicense and MIT licenses. No concrete malicious or supply-chain indicators were present in this file: there were no install hooks, network/exfiltration behavior, credential access, dynamic code loading, obfuscation, or persistence logic to review.

Reviewed the GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/ci.yml. It defines CI jobs that check out the repo, install Rust, optionally install `cross` for cross-compilation, and run build/doc/test/formatting checks; I did not find concrete indicators of malicious behavior such as secret harvesting, exfiltration, hidden downloads beyond the expected tool installs, obfuscation, or persistence.

Reviewed `src/packed/teddy/mod.rs`, which is a small architecture-gated module shim that re-exports `Builder`/`Teddy` and provides a non-x86_64 fallback with no-op methods. I checked for install-time execution, network or exfiltration behavior, credential access, dynamic code loading, obfuscation, and persistence in this file and found no concrete malicious or supply-chain indicators.
